### REQUEST ###
```
POST https://api2.cursor.sh:443/aiserver.v1.RepositoryService/FastUpdateFileV2 HTTP/1.1
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Authorization: Bearer REDACTED
Connect-Protocol-Version: 1
Connect-Timeout-Ms: 180000
Connection: close
Content-Encoding: gzip
Content-Type: application/proto
Host: api2.cursor.sh
Traceparent: REDACTED
User-Agent: connect-es/1.6.1
X-Amzn-Trace-Id: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Checksum: REDACTED

X-Cursor-Client-Device-Type: desktop
X-Cursor-Client-Os: win32
X-Cursor-Client-Type: ide
X-Cursor-Client-Version: 2.6.11
X-Cursor-Config-Version: REDACTED
X-Cursor-Timezone: REDACTED

X-New-Onboarding-Completed: false

X-Session-Id: REDACTED

```
{
  "1:LEN": {
    "1:I64": 4842195278606858022
  },
  "2:LEN": "REDACTED",
  "6:VARINT": 4,
  "7:LEN": [
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport asyncio\r\nimport signal\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List\r\n\r\nimport yaml  # type: ignore[import-untyped]\r\nfrom prometheus_client import start_http_server\r\nfrom sqlalchemy import and_, select\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\nfrom .broker import enqueue_job, update_queue_depth\r\nfrom .config import get_settings\r\nfrom .db import get_session_factory, init_db\r\nfrom .logging import configure_logging, get_logger\r\nfrom .metrics import SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL\r\nfrom .models import Job, JobState, ScheduleTick\r\nfrom .state_machine import transition_state\r\n\r\n\r\nlogger = get_logger(__name__, service=\"scheduler\")\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass RecurringSchedule:\r\n    id: str\r\n    job_type: str\r\n    queue: str\r\n    interval_seconds: int\r\n    max_attempts: int\r\n    payload: Dict[str, Any]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_schedules(path: Path) -> List[RecurringSchedule]:\r\n    if not path.exists():\r\n        return []\r\n    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")) or {}\r\n    out: List[RecurringSchedule] = []\r\n    for item in raw.get(\"schedules\", []):\r\n        out.append(\r\n            RecurringSchedule(\r\n                id=str(item[\"id\"]),\r\n                job_type=str(item[\"job_type\"]),\r\n                queue=str(item.get(\"queue\", \"default\")),\r\n                interval_seconds=int(item.get(\"interval_seconds\", 60)),\r\n                max_attempts=int(item.get(\"max_attempts\", 3)),\r\n                payload=dict(item.get(\"payload\", {})),\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n    return out\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def _enqueue_due_jobs(session: AsyncSession, now: datetime) -> None:\r\n    \"\"\"Enqueue delayed/retry jobs whose next_run_at is due.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    stmt = (\r\n        select(Job)\r\n        .where(\r\n            and_(\r\n                Job.state == JobState.QUEUED,\r\n                Job.is_enqueued.is_(False),\r\n                Job.next_run_at.is_not(None),\r\n                Job.next_run_at <= now,\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n        .with_for_update(skip_locked=True)\r\n    )\r\n    result = await session.execute(stmt)\r\n    jobs: List[Job] = list(result.scalars())\r\n    for job in jobs:\r\n        await enqueue_job(\r\n            job_id=job.id,\r\n            job_type=job.type,\r\n            queue=job.queue,\r\n            idempotency_key=job.idempotency_key,\r\n        )\r\n        job.is_enqueued = True\r\n        job.next_run_at = None\r\n        await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Scheduler enqueued delayed job\")\r\n        SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL.labels(schedule_id=\"delayed\").inc()\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def _enqueue_recurring(\r\n    session: AsyncSession,\r\n    schedules: List[RecurringSchedule],\r\n    now: datetime,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    \"\"\"Enqueue recurring jobs based on config file schedules.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    for sched in schedules:\r\n        # Compute the current tick's planned_time by flooring now to interval.\r\n        seconds = int(now.timestamp())\r\n        tick_start = seconds - (seconds % sched.interval_seconds)\r\n        planned = datetime.fromtimestamp(tick_start, tz=timezone.utc)\r\n\r\n        # Check if this tick already exists.\r\n        stmt = select(ScheduleTick).where(\r\n            and_(\r\n                ScheduleTick.schedule_id == sched.id,\r\n                ScheduleTick.planned_time == planned,\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n        exists = (await session.execute(stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()\r\n        if exists:\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        tick = ScheduleTick(schedule_id=sched.id, planned_time=planned)\r\n        session.add(tick)\r\n        await session.flush()\r\n\r\n        # Create a new job row for this schedule.\r\n        job = Job(\r\n            type=sched.job_type,\r\n            queue=sched.queue,\r\n            payload=sched.payload,\r\n            max_attempts=sched.max_attempts,\r\n            state=JobState.QUEUED,\r\n            is_enqueued=False,\r\n            next_run_at=now,\r\n            schedule=sched.id,\r\n            idempotency_key=f\"{sched.id}:{planned.isoformat()}\",\r\n        )\r\n        session.add(job)\r\n        await session.flush()\r\n\r\n        await enqueue_job(\r\n            job_id=job.id,\r\n            job_type=job.type,\r\n            queue=job.queue,\r\n            idempotency_key=job.idempotency_key,\r\n        )\r\n        job.is_enqueued = True\r\n        await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Scheduler enqueued recurring job\")\r\n        SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL.labels(schedule_id=sched.id).inc()\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def scheduler_loop(schedules: List[RecurringSchedule]) -> None:\r\n    settings = get_settings()\r\n    factory = get_session_factory()\r\n    queue = settings.queue_default\r\n\r\n    while True:\r\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n        async with factory() as session:\r\n            async with session.begin():\r\n                await _enqueue_due_jobs(session, now)\r\n                await _enqueue_recurring(session, schedules, now)\r\n        try:\r\n            await update_queue_depth(queue)\r\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n            logger.exception(\"Failed to update queue depth\")\r\n        await asyncio.sleep(settings.scheduler_interval_sec)\r\n\r\n\r\nasync def main() -> None:\r\n    configure_logging(service=\"scheduler\")\r\n    settings = get_settings()\r\n\r\n    # Prometheus metrics server for scheduler.\r\n    start_http_server(settings.metrics_port)\r\n\r\n    await init_db()\r\n\r\n    config_path = Path(\"config\") / \"schedules.yml\"\r\n    schedules = load_schedules(config_path)\r\n    logger.info(\"Loaded schedules\", extra={\"count\": len(schedules)})\r\n\r\n    stop_event = asyncio.Event()\r\n\r\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\r\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\r\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, stop_event.set)\r\n\r\n    task = asyncio.create_task(scheduler_loop(schedules))\r\n\r\n    await stop_event.wait()\r\n    task.cancel()\r\n    await asyncio.gather(task, return_exceptions=True)\r\n\r\n\r\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\r\n    asyncio.run(main())\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "d3b57316c58958984af945aa51475d7b345b3f286594abb13d4e795a90f4b5b2",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },

        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    },
    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport enum\r\nimport uuid\r\nfrom datetime import datetime\r\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n\r\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, Boolean, DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, Integer, String, Text, UniqueConstraint\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\r\n\r\n\r\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\r\n    pass\r\n\r\n\r\nclass JobState(str, enum.Enum):\r\n    QUEUED = \"QUEUED\"\r\n    RUNNING = \"RUNNING\"\r\n    SUCCEEDED = \"SUCCEEDED\"\r\n    FAILED = \"FAILED\"\r\n    CANCELED = \"CANCELED\"\r\n    DEAD = \"DEAD\"\r\n\r\n\r\nclass Job(Base):\r\n    __tablename__ = \"jobs\"\r\n\r\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\r\n    )\r\n    type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False)\r\n    queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), default=\"default\")\r\n\r\n    payload: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(\r\n        Enum(JobState), default=JobState.QUEUED, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    idempotency_key: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0, nullable=False)\r\n    max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    next_run_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    schedule: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    last_error: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    result: Mapped[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    is_enqueued: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n    cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    events: Mapped[list[\"JobEvent\"]] = relationship(\r\n        back_populates=\"job\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\nclass JobEvent(Base):\r\n    __tablename__ = \"job_events\"\r\n\r\n    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n\r\n    job_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"jobs.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    ts: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    old_state: Mapped[Optional[JobState]] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=True)\r\n    new_state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    message: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    # Use attribute name `data` but persist as column `metadata` to avoid\r\n    # colliding with SQLAlchemy's declarative .metadata attribute.\r\n    data: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(\"metadata\", JSON, default=dict, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    job: Mapped[Job] = relationship(back_populates=\"events\")\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ScheduleTick(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"schedule_ticks\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     schedule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=False)\r\n     planned_time: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)\r\n     enqueued_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     __table_args__ = (\r\n         UniqueConstraint(\"schedule_id\", \"planned_time\", name=\"uq_schedule_tick\"),\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "e4525739fa79d4ec52038eed272e5ea08f63d21ee8f542cee6b17a31197233c6",

    {
      "2:LEN": {
        "1:LEN": {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED",
          "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport logging\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, MutableMapping, Optional, Type\r\n\r\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\nfrom ..config import Settings, get_settings\r\nfrom ..models import Job\r\n\r\n\r\nTaskFunc = Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]]\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TaskDefinition:\r\n    name: str\r\n    payload_model: Type[BaseModel]\r\n    func: TaskFunc\r\n    max_attempts_default: int\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TaskContext:\r\n    job: Job\r\n    attempt: int\r\n    session: Optional[AsyncSession]\r\n    settings: Settings\r\n    logger: logging.Logger\r\n    check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]]\r\n\r\n\r\n_TASKS: MutableMapping[str, TaskDefinition] = {}\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register_task(\r\n    name: str,\r\n    *,\r\n    payload_model: Type[BaseModel],\r\n    max_attempts_default: int = 3,\r\n) -> Callable[[TaskFunc], TaskFunc]:\r\n    \"\"\"Decorator to register a task implementation.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    def decorator(func: TaskFunc) -> TaskFunc:\r\n        if name in _TASKS:\r\n            raise ValueError(f\"Task {name!r} already registered\")\r\n        _TASKS[name] = TaskDefinition(\r\n            name=name,\r\n            payload_model=payload_model,\r\n            func=func,\r\n            max_attempts_default=max_attempts_default,\r\n        )\r\n        return func\r\n\r\n    return decorator\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_task_definition(name: str) -> TaskDefinition:\r\n    try:\r\n        return _TASKS[name]\r\n    except KeyError as exc:\r\n        raise KeyError(f\"Unknown task type {name!r}\") from exc\r\n\r\n\r\ndef make_context(\r\n    *,\r\n    job: Job,\r\n    attempt: int,\r\n    session: Optional[AsyncSession],\r\n    logger: logging.Logger,\r\n    check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]],\r\n) -> TaskContext:\r\n    return TaskContext(\r\n        job=job,\r\n        attempt=attempt,\r\n        session=session,\r\n        settings=get_settings(),\r\n        logger=logger,\r\n        check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n    )\r\n\r\n"
        },
        "2:LEN": "eb5b943f43bd7eb5f83a5540f74eb2c7407bc3b857557107a7b73100b496fc91",
        "3:LEN": "REDACTED"
      },
      "3:LEN": [
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "REDACTED"
        },
        {
          "1:LEN": "."
        }
      ],
      "4:VARINT": 1
    }
  ]
}
```

### RESPONSE ###
```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Grpc-Status\, Grpc-Message\, Grpc-Status-Details-Bin\, Content-Encoding\, Connect-Content-Encoding\, traceparent\, backend-traceparent\, x-amzn-trace-id\, x-request-id
Content-Type: application/proto
Date: Thu\, 05 Mar 2026 03:31:33 GMT
Vary: Origin
```

{

}

### SIGNATURE ###
```
http:host is localhost
http:method is POST
http:queryparams is -NONE-
http:requestBodyJSON is {"1:LEN": {"1:I64": 4842195278606858022}, "2:LEN": "REDACTED", "6:VARINT": 4, "7:LEN": [{"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport asyncio\r\nimport signal\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\r\nfrom pathlib import Path\r\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List\r\n\r\nimport yaml  # type: ignore[import-untyped]\r\nfrom prometheus_client import start_http_server\r\nfrom sqlalchemy import and_, select\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\nfrom .broker import enqueue_job, update_queue_depth\r\nfrom .config import get_settings\r\nfrom .db import get_session_factory, init_db\r\nfrom .logging import configure_logging, get_logger\r\nfrom .metrics import SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL\r\nfrom .models import Job, JobState, ScheduleTick\r\nfrom .state_machine import transition_state\r\n\r\n\r\nlogger = get_logger(__name__, service=\"scheduler\")\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass RecurringSchedule:\r\n    id: str\r\n    job_type: str\r\n    queue: str\r\n    interval_seconds: int\r\n    max_attempts: int\r\n    payload: Dict[str, Any]\r\n\r\n\r\ndef load_schedules(path: Path) -> List[RecurringSchedule]:\r\n    if not path.exists():\r\n        return []\r\n    raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")) or {}\r\n    out: List[RecurringSchedule] = []\r\n    for item in raw.get(\"schedules\", []):\r\n        out.append(\r\n            RecurringSchedule(\r\n                id=str(item[\"id\"]),\r\n                job_type=str(item[\"job_type\"]),\r\n                queue=str(item.get(\"queue\", \"default\")),\r\n                interval_seconds=int(item.get(\"interval_seconds\", 60)),\r\n                max_attempts=int(item.get(\"max_attempts\", 3)),\r\n                payload=dict(item.get(\"payload\", {})),\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n    return out\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def _enqueue_due_jobs(session: AsyncSession, now: datetime) -> None:\r\n    \"\"\"Enqueue delayed/retry jobs whose next_run_at is due.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    stmt = (\r\n        select(Job)\r\n        .where(\r\n            and_(\r\n                Job.state == JobState.QUEUED,\r\n                Job.is_enqueued.is_(False),\r\n                Job.next_run_at.is_not(None),\r\n                Job.next_run_at <= now,\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n        .with_for_update(skip_locked=True)\r\n    )\r\n    result = await session.execute(stmt)\r\n    jobs: List[Job] = list(result.scalars())\r\n    for job in jobs:\r\n        await enqueue_job(\r\n            job_id=job.id,\r\n            job_type=job.type,\r\n            queue=job.queue,\r\n            idempotency_key=job.idempotency_key,\r\n        )\r\n        job.is_enqueued = True\r\n        job.next_run_at = None\r\n        await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Scheduler enqueued delayed job\")\r\n        SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL.labels(schedule_id=\"delayed\").inc()\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def _enqueue_recurring(\r\n    session: AsyncSession,\r\n    schedules: List[RecurringSchedule],\r\n    now: datetime,\r\n) -> None:\r\n    \"\"\"Enqueue recurring jobs based on config file schedules.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    for sched in schedules:\r\n        # Compute the current tick's planned_time by flooring now to interval.\r\n        seconds = int(now.timestamp())\r\n        tick_start = seconds - (seconds % sched.interval_seconds)\r\n        planned = datetime.fromtimestamp(tick_start, tz=timezone.utc)\r\n\r\n        # Check if this tick already exists.\r\n        stmt = select(ScheduleTick).where(\r\n            and_(\r\n                ScheduleTick.schedule_id == sched.id,\r\n                ScheduleTick.planned_time == planned,\r\n            )\r\n        )\r\n        exists = (await session.execute(stmt)).scalar_one_or_none()\r\n        if exists:\r\n            continue\r\n\r\n        tick = ScheduleTick(schedule_id=sched.id, planned_time=planned)\r\n        session.add(tick)\r\n        await session.flush()\r\n\r\n        # Create a new job row for this schedule.\r\n        job = Job(\r\n            type=sched.job_type,\r\n            queue=sched.queue,\r\n            payload=sched.payload,\r\n            max_attempts=sched.max_attempts,\r\n            state=JobState.QUEUED,\r\n            is_enqueued=False,\r\n            next_run_at=now,\r\n            schedule=sched.id,\r\n            idempotency_key=f\"{sched.id}:{planned.isoformat()}\",\r\n        )\r\n        session.add(job)\r\n        await session.flush()\r\n\r\n        await enqueue_job(\r\n            job_id=job.id,\r\n            job_type=job.type,\r\n            queue=job.queue,\r\n            idempotency_key=job.idempotency_key,\r\n        )\r\n        job.is_enqueued = True\r\n        await transition_state(session, job, JobState.QUEUED, \"Scheduler enqueued recurring job\")\r\n        SCHEDULER_ENQUEUES_TOTAL.labels(schedule_id=sched.id).inc()\r\n \r\n \r\nasync def scheduler_loop(schedules: List[RecurringSchedule]) -> None:\r\n    settings = get_settings()\r\n    factory = get_session_factory()\r\n    queue = settings.queue_default\r\n\r\n    while True:\r\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n        async with factory() as session:\r\n            async with session.begin():\r\n                await _enqueue_due_jobs(session, now)\r\n                await _enqueue_recurring(session, schedules, now)\r\n        try:\r\n            await update_queue_depth(queue)\r\n        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001\r\n            logger.exception(\"Failed to update queue depth\")\r\n        await asyncio.sleep(settings.scheduler_interval_sec)\r\n\r\n\r\nasync def main() -> None:\r\n    configure_logging(service=\"scheduler\")\r\n    settings = get_settings()\r\n\r\n    # Prometheus metrics server for scheduler.\r\n    start_http_server(settings.metrics_port)\r\n\r\n    await init_db()\r\n\r\n    config_path = Path(\"config\") / \"schedules.yml\"\r\n    schedules = load_schedules(config_path)\r\n    logger.info(\"Loaded schedules\", extra={\"count\": len(schedules)})\r\n\r\n    stop_event = asyncio.Event()\r\n\r\n    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()\r\n    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\r\n        loop.add_signal_handler(sig, stop_event.set)\r\n\r\n    task = asyncio.create_task(scheduler_loop(schedules))\r\n\r\n    await stop_event.wait()\r\n    task.cancel()\r\n    await asyncio.gather(task, return_exceptions=True)\r\n\r\n\r\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\r\n    asyncio.run(main())\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "d3b57316c58958984af945aa51475d7b345b3f286594abb13d4e795a90f4b5b2", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport enum\r\nimport uuid\r\nfrom datetime import datetime\r\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\r\n\r\nfrom sqlalchemy import JSON, Boolean, DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, Integer, String, Text, UniqueConstraint\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, Mapped, mapped_column, relationship\r\n\r\n\r\nclass Base(DeclarativeBase):\r\n    pass\r\n\r\n\r\nclass JobState(str, enum.Enum):\r\n    QUEUED = \"QUEUED\"\r\n    RUNNING = \"RUNNING\"\r\n    SUCCEEDED = \"SUCCEEDED\"\r\n    FAILED = \"FAILED\"\r\n    CANCELED = \"CANCELED\"\r\n    DEAD = \"DEAD\"\r\n\r\n\r\nclass Job(Base):\r\n    __tablename__ = \"jobs\"\r\n\r\n    id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n        String(36), primary_key=True, default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())\r\n    )\r\n    type: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), nullable=False)\r\n    queue: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(64), default=\"default\")\r\n\r\n    payload: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(\r\n        Enum(JobState), default=JobState.QUEUED, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n    updated_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    idempotency_key: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0, nullable=False)\r\n    max_attempts: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=3, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    next_run_at: Mapped[Optional[datetime]] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    schedule: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    last_error: Mapped[Optional[str]] = mapped_column(Text, nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    result: Mapped[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]] = mapped_column(JSON, nullable=True)\r\n\r\n    is_enqueued: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n    cancel_requested: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    events: Mapped[list[\"JobEvent\"]] = relationship(\r\n        back_populates=\"job\", cascade=\"all, delete-orphan\"\r\n    )\r\n\r\n\r\nclass JobEvent(Base):\r\n    __tablename__ = \"job_events\"\r\n\r\n    id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n\r\n    job_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(\r\n        String(36), ForeignKey(\"jobs.id\", ondelete=\"CASCADE\"), nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    ts: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n        DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    old_state: Mapped[Optional[JobState]] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=True)\r\n    new_state: Mapped[JobState] = mapped_column(Enum(JobState), nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    message: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255), nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    # Use attribute name `data` but persist as column `metadata` to avoid\r\n    # colliding with SQLAlchemy's declarative .metadata attribute.\r\n    data: Mapped[Dict[str, Any]] = mapped_column(\"metadata\", JSON, default=dict, nullable=False)\r\n\r\n    job: Mapped[Job] = relationship(back_populates=\"events\")\r\n\r\n\r\nclass ScheduleTick(Base):\r\n     __tablename__ = \"schedule_ticks\"\r\n \r\n     id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)\r\n \r\n     schedule_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(128), nullable=False)\r\n     planned_time: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)\r\n     enqueued_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column(\r\n         DateTime(timezone=True), default=datetime.utcnow, nullable=False\r\n     )\r\n \r\n     __table_args__ = (\r\n         UniqueConstraint(\"schedule_id\", \"planned_time\", name=\"uq_schedule_tick\"),\r\n     )\r\n \r\n"}, "2:LEN": "e4525739fa79d4ec52038eed272e5ea08f63d21ee8f542cee6b17a31197233c6", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}, {"2:LEN": {"1:LEN": {"1:LEN": "REDACTED", "2:LEN": "from __future__ import annotations\r\n\r\nimport logging\r\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\r\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, MutableMapping, Optional, Type\r\n\r\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\r\nfrom sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession\r\n\r\nfrom ..config import Settings, get_settings\r\nfrom ..models import Job\r\n\r\n\r\nTaskFunc = Callable[..., Awaitable[Any]]\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TaskDefinition:\r\n    name: str\r\n    payload_model: Type[BaseModel]\r\n    func: TaskFunc\r\n    max_attempts_default: int\r\n\r\n\r\n@dataclass\r\nclass TaskContext:\r\n    job: Job\r\n    attempt: int\r\n    session: Optional[AsyncSession]\r\n    settings: Settings\r\n    logger: logging.Logger\r\n    check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]]\r\n\r\n\r\n_TASKS: MutableMapping[str, TaskDefinition] = {}\r\n\r\n\r\ndef register_task(\r\n    name: str,\r\n    *,\r\n    payload_model: Type[BaseModel],\r\n    max_attempts_default: int = 3,\r\n) -> Callable[[TaskFunc], TaskFunc]:\r\n    \"\"\"Decorator to register a task implementation.\"\"\"\r\n\r\n    def decorator(func: TaskFunc) -> TaskFunc:\r\n        if name in _TASKS:\r\n            raise ValueError(f\"Task {name!r} already registered\")\r\n        _TASKS[name] = TaskDefinition(\r\n            name=name,\r\n            payload_model=payload_model,\r\n            func=func,\r\n            max_attempts_default=max_attempts_default,\r\n        )\r\n        return func\r\n\r\n    return decorator\r\n\r\n\r\ndef get_task_definition(name: str) -> TaskDefinition:\r\n    try:\r\n        return _TASKS[name]\r\n    except KeyError as exc:\r\n        raise KeyError(f\"Unknown task type {name!r}\") from exc\r\n\r\n\r\ndef make_context(\r\n    *,\r\n    job: Job,\r\n    attempt: int,\r\n    session: Optional[AsyncSession],\r\n    logger: logging.Logger,\r\n    check_canceled: Callable[[], Awaitable[None]],\r\n) -> TaskContext:\r\n    return TaskContext(\r\n        job=job,\r\n        attempt=attempt,\r\n        session=session,\r\n        settings=get_settings(),\r\n        logger=logger,\r\n        check_canceled=check_canceled,\r\n    )\r\n\r\n"}, "2:LEN": "eb5b943f43bd7eb5f83a5540f74eb2c7407bc3b857557107a7b73100b496fc91", "3:LEN": "REDACTED"}, "3:LEN": [{"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "REDACTED"}, {"1:LEN": "."}], "4:VARINT": 1}]}
